title: Lord of the Rings The Fellowship of the Ring
author: J R R Tolkien
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "Lord of the Rings The Fellowship of the Ring" book by J R R Tolkien?
Options:
A. There were recesses in the rock walls holding big wooden chests bound in iron. The chests were all broken and emptied of valuables. Beside one with a shattered lid was a ruined book. It was cut up, stabbed, burned somewhat, and covered in stains like old blood so that barely anything could be read. Gandalf gently picked up the book, but its pages split and broke when he put it on the stone. He looked over it quietly for a while as Frodo and Gimli observed. They saw that the pages were written in various runic alphabets of Moria and Dale, plus Elvish script, by many hands.
B. Numerous niches were carved into the rock walls, containing large wooden chests reinforced with iron bands. The chests had all been smashed open and looted. Next to one with a broken lid lay the remains of a book. It was slashed, stabbed, partially burned, and so stained with marks resembling dried blood that little text could be deciphered. Gandalf carefully lifted the book, but its pages cracked and tore when he set it down. He studied it wordlessly for a time as Frodo and Gimli watched. They noticed the pages were written in different runic alphabets of Moria and Dale, as well as Elvish script, by many writers.
C. There were many recesses cut in the rock of the walls, and in them were large iron-bound chests of wood. All had been broken and plundered; but beside the shattered lid of one there lay the remains of a book. It had been slashed and stabbed and partly burned, and it was so stained with black and other dark marks like old blood that little of it could be read. Gandalf lifted it carefully, but the leaves cracked and broke as he laid it on the slab. He pored over it for some time without speaking. Frodo and Gimli standing at his side could see, as he gingerly turned the leaves, that they were written by many different hands, in runes, both of Moria and of Dale, and here and there in Elvish script.
D. The walls had many recesses containing large wooden chests reinforced with iron bands. All the chests had been broken into and robbed. Next to one chest with a smashed lid was a damaged book. It was slashed, stabbed, partly burned, and stained so much with marks resembling old blood that little could be read. Gandalf carefully lifted the book, but its pages cracked and tore as he set it down. He studied it silently for some time, while Frodo and Gimli watched. They could see that many hands had written the pages in different runic scripts of Moria and Dale, as well as in Elvish. 
Answer:

It was cut up, stabbed, burned somewhat, and covered in stains like old blood so that barely anything could be read. They saw that the pages were written in various runic alphabets of Moria and Dale, plus Elvish script, by many hands.

It had been slashed and stabbed and partly burned, and it was so stained with black and other dark marks like old blood that little of it could be read. Gandalf lifted it carefully, but the leaves cracked and broke as he laid it on the slab. He pored over it for some time without speaking. Frodo and Gimli standing at his side could see, as he gingerly turned the leaves, that they were written by many different hands, in runes, both of Moria and of Dale, and here and there in Elvish script.
options.D: The walls had many recesses containing large wooden chests reinforced with iron bands. All the chests had been broken into and robbed. Next to one chest with a smashed lid was a damaged book. It was slashed, stabbed, partly burned, and stained so much with marks resembling old blood that little could be read. They could see that many hands had written the pages in different runic scripts of Moria and Dale, as well as in Elvish.
